questions.question: A hospital needs to mix $2$ liters of a $10%$ saline solution with a $20%$ saline solution to obtain $4$ liters of a $15%$ saline solution. How many liters of the $20%$ solution are needed?

A patient is prescribed a medicine that contains $25$ mg of active ingredient per $100$ mg of solution. If the patient is to take $50$ mg of active ingredient, how many milligrams of the solution must be taken?
A cell culture contains $15%$ of a specific cell type. If $20%$ of the cells are to be transferred to a new culture, what percentage of the new culture will be of the specific cell type?
A pharmacist mixes $300$ grams of a $10%$ ointment with $400$ grams of a $20%$ ointment. What is the concentration of the resulting mixture?
A patient's medication is $30%$ effective for $40%$ of patients. What percentage of patients can expect to be helped by the medication?

A researcher is testing the effect of a new drug on cell growth. The control group has a $20%$ growth rate, while the experimental group has a $30%$ growth rate. If the experimental group has $25%$ more cells than the control group, what is the ratio of the experimental group's growth rate to the control group's growth rate?

A hospital has $15$ doses of a $25%$ concentration of a medication and $20$ doses of a $30%$ concentration. If the hospital needs to mix the doses to obtain a $28%$ concentration, how many doses of the $25%$ concentration should be used?
A medical laboratory has a $10%$ solution and a $50%$ solution of a reagent. How many liters of the $50%$ solution should be mixed with $5$ liters of the $10%$ solution to obtain a $25%$ solution?

In a clinical trial, $80%$ of patients respond to a new medication. If $60%$ of the patients are male, what is the probability that a patient is male and responds to the medication? Show your work and explain your reasoning.

A doctor mixes $2$ liters of a $10%$ saline solution with $x$ liters of a $20%$ saline solution to obtain $3$ liters of a $15%$ saline solution. Set up an equation and solve for $x$. Show your work and explain your reasoning.

teacher_rules: Read each question carefully and choose the correct answer.
For multiple-choice questions, select only one answer.
For free-response questions, show your work and explain your reasoning.
